verify_reason: Independent re-measurement reproduces every load-bearing number. (1) Exactly 1434 `pub fn` in crates/cas_math/src (regex `^\s*pub fn`). (2) With an .rs-only whole-workspace token universe (2597 files walked; target/.git excluded), 333 of 1434 (23.2%) have zero word-boundary token occurrences in any file other than their defining file — claim said 332/23%, an immaterial off-by-one. (3) Per-file hotspots match: number_theory_support.rs 33, summation_support.rs 21, div_expand_cancel_support.rs 15, trig_canonicalization_support.rs 14, trig_contraction_support.rs 14 (limits_support.rs 22 vs claimed 21). (4) Methodology is sound for this codebase: no `paste!`/`concat_idents` anywhere in crates/, so token scans cannot miss macro-generated identifier references; the scan includes tests/benches/examples/scripts. (5) The lint-blindness mechanism is real: cas_math is a library consumed only via workspace path-deps (cas_engine, cas_solver, cas_solver_core, cas_didactic, cas_cli), and rustc's dead_code lint exempts `pub` items, so none of these 333 file-local functions would ever warn if unused — my scan independently found 12 with zero token refs anywhere beyond their definition site (claim cross-references 15; same order, methodology-dependent), proving the blind spot is already operative, e.g. `is_first_quadrant` (trig_table.rs:94) and `mk_inf` (limits_support.rs:112). Secondary evidence also checks out: rayon is optional and gated behind the default-on `parallel` feature; lib.rs declares 196 mods matching disk exactly (194 root-file mods + 2 dir mods). If documentation files (.md/.json) are counted as references the number drops to 274 (19.1%) — still material. Cargo deps are 8 including the path-dep cas_ast (claim said 7, presumably counting external-only): cas_ast, rustc-hash, rayon(optional), num-rational, num-traits, num-bigint, num-integer, smallvec — all plausibly used. Fully-dead candidates (zero token refs anywhere beyond definition) by my count: 12, including allows_method_probe/allows_verification_check (general_integration_backend/probe_runner.rs), eval_var_zero (mono.rs), from_var (multipoly/mod.rs), is_first_quadrant (trig_table.rs), mk_inf (limits_support.rs), same_variables (poly_modp_conv.rs), with_steps (substitute.rs). Note for the proposed fix: pub(crate) is sufficient to re-enable dead_code lint (it fires on unreachable pub(crate) items), and for the 333 file-local fns plain private would work; the change is behavior-neutral so huella-safe as claimed.

title: Exact surd/nth-root value comparison implemented 3x across cas_solver / cas_solver_core / cas_math, with confirmed inter-copy drift
evidence: Three parallel exact comparators, all read: (1) cas_solver/src/solve_backend_local.rs:1330 cmp_rational_to_quadratic_surd, :1365 cmp_rational_to_nth_root, plus nested decomposers bound_surd (:1419) and bound_nth_root (:1436); (2) cas_solver_core/src/solution_set.rs:108 as_surd_value, :117 sign_of_linear_surd, :143 sign_of_sum_two_surds, :207 compare_quadratic_surds, :230 as_nth_root_value, :286 compare_positive_nth_roots, :306 compare_nth_root_surds; (3) cas_math/src/root_forms.rs:1739 as_linear_surd, :1876 provable_sign_vs_zero. cmp_rational_to_quadratic_surd(r,a,b,n) is algorithmically sign_of_linear_surd(r-a,-b,n) — the same square-with-sign-tracking discipline maintained twice. Confirmed drift: (a) bound_nth_root (:1436) handles only Neg and Pow(rational,1/n), while as_nth_root_value (solution_set.rs:230) additionally handles a rational Mul coefficient `c*q^(1/n)` and plain rationals — so the soundness gate rational_inequality_candidate_verifies (:1402) declines candidates whose bounds compare_values CAN order; (b) bound_surd (:1425-1432) recognizes both `Phi` and `Neg(Phi)`, but as_surd_value (solution_set.rs:108) recognizes only bare `Phi` (as_linear_surd's Neg branch recurses into Phi and returns None, verified at root_forms.rs:1784), so a `-Phi` interval endpoint falls to the value-blind structural compare in compare_values.
why: Memory records surd-sign helpers as THE chokepoint for P0 wrong-answer families (2026-07-01 campaign fixed families by upgrading them). With three parallel comparators, the next sign-guard upgrade must be applied three times or a fixed family silently reopens on one path (already happening: the Mul-coefficient nth-root form is orderable in interval algebra but not in the inequality verification gate). This is not deliberate layering: cas_solver_core already depends on cas_math (isolation_utils.rs:41 calls root_forms), and solve_backend_local.rs:22 doc-comments the parallel explicitly.
fix: Canonicalize in cas_math::root_forms: export sign_of_linear_surd, sign_of_sum_two_surds, cmp_rational_to_nth_root, compare_positive_nth_roots (pure BigRational, no Context needed) plus one as_surd_value/as_nth_root_value decomposer that includes Phi/Neg(Phi) and the Mul-coefficient case. Make solution_set.rs and solve_backend_local.rs delegate. Pure delegation is huella-safe; unifying the drifted decomposers only WIDENS previously-declining cases — land that part with the boundary sweep discipline (include c=0 / negated / coefficient siblings) and scorecard-huella validation.
verify_reason: Every checkable assertion verified against source. Four (not three) parallel implementations of the exact surd-sign algorithm exist: root_forms.rs:1876 provable_sign_vs_zero, solution_set.rs:117 sign_of_linear_surd, solve_backend_local.rs:28 linear_surd_sign, and solve_backend_local.rs:1330 cmp_rational_to_quadratic_surd (verified algebraically identical to sign_of_linear_surd(r-a,-b,n)). Both claimed drifts are real: (a) bound_nth_root (solve_backend_local.rs:1436) matches only Neg/Pow while as_nth_root_value (solution_set.rs:230) also handles rational Mul coefficients and plain rationals, so the verification gate declines bounds like 2*2^(1/3) that compare_values can order; (b) bound_surd (:1425-1431) handles Phi and Neg(Phi) but as_surd_value (solution_set.rs:108) only bare Phi — as_linear_surd has no Constant::Phi arm (falls to _ => None at root_forms.rs:1865), so Neg(Phi) returns None via the Neg recursion at :1784. Not deliberate layering: cas_solver_core/Cargo.toml:12 depends on cas_math and isolation_utils.rs:41 already calls root_forms directly, and solve_backend_local.rs:22 doc-comments the parallel ('the same exact surd-sign logic as provable_sign_vs_zero') without justifying it. P1 is correct: divergence confirmed (raises severity above cosmetic dup) but neither drift yields a wrong answer — both fail toward declining or structural fallback — so P0 is not warranted.
extra_evidence: The finding UNDERCOUNTS: a fourth copy, linear_surd_sign at crates/cas_solver/src/solve_backend_local.rs:28-53, implements the identical algorithm and its doc-comment (line 22) explicitly names root_forms::provable_sign_vs_zero as the same logic; it should be included in the proposed consolidation. Minor mitigation to note for drift (b): compare_values (solution_set.rs:89-99) has a downstream const_value_bounds branch and const_sign.rs:419 does handle Phi, so a -Phi endpoint still orders correctly when value-bound intervals SEPARATE; the exact path only matters for touching/equal comparisons. Drift (a)'s practical effect is over-declining (rational_inequality_candidate_verifies returns false -> candidate=None -> prior behavior), i.e. capability loss not unsoundness. Proposed fix direction (canonicalize in cas_math::root_forms, delegate from both consumers, land decomposer-widening with boundary-sweep + huella discipline) is consistent with the repo's documented architecture lessons and dependency graph.

title: server_colab.py is a hand-forked copy of server.py that has drifted into a fully broken state (2 confirmed breakages)
evidence: web/server_colab.py duplicates ~230 lines of web/server.py verbatim (_split_top_level_pair 118-136 vs server.py:55-74, _parse_special_pair_command 138-153 vs 76-91, _merge_equiv_with_derive_steps 168-184 vs 106-151, the 4 _coerce_* helpers 61-105 vs 195-265, call_cas_cli 353-430 vs 873-958). fix: Pick one: delete web/server_colab.py (it cannot currently run, so nothing depends on it), or repair the two broken lines and extract the shared block (_coerce_* helpers, pair/equiv parsing, _merge_equiv_with_derive_steps, call_cas_cli argv construction) into a web/server_common.py imported by both — app_config.py already establishes exactly this sharing pattern. Canonical copy: web/server.py.
verify_reason: Every factual claim reproduced by execution: (a) _coerce_domain_mode('strict'/'generic') returns None (missing return, server_colab.py:75-81) while index.html:1899 always sends a non-empty domain, so every /api/eval would embed None in the subprocess argv and fail inside call_cas_cli's broad except (line 429); (b) main() crashes at startup — ran it: TypeError: env_int() got multiple values for argument 'default' (server_colab.py:473 vs app_config.env_int(name, default) at app_config.py:55); (c) unreachable duplicated return value at lines 103-105; (d) drift confirmed — server.py has _coerce_language (222), _filter_plumbing_steps (728), _present_original_input (744), _file_lock snapshot locking (167/702), and the wire.messages rewrite in _merge_equiv_with_derive_steps (122-150), all absent from the colab fork, while ~230 lines remain verbatim-duplicated. Not deliberate layering: docs/ENGINE_COMBINATION_LEDGER.md:2513 states server_colab.py parity 'is pending', i.e. the repo intends to keep the fork in sync by hand and has failed. extra_evidence: Executed: `python3 -c "import server_colab; server_colab._coerce_domain_mode('strict')"` -> None; `server_colab.main()` -> TypeError: env_int() got multiple values for argument 'default'. Only repo reference to the file is docs/ENGINE_COMBINATION_LEDGER.md (lines 2291, 2443, 2513 — the last admits parity is pending). Nothing launches it (its own docstring line 13 tells pm2 to run web/server.py), so the delete option in the proposed fix is safe.

title: solve_runtime_flow_* callback plumbing: 48 functions with >=10 params (max 41 params / 35 generic callback types) forwarded across 61 files
evidence: Measured programmatically over crates/cas_solver_core/src/solve_runtime_*.rs (61 files): 48 fns with >=10 params, 31 with >=15. Worst: dispatch_isolation_with_default_kernels_and_default_arithmetic_pow_function_routes_with_state (solve_runtime_flow_isolation_default_routes.rs:15) = 41 params, 35 generic type params, ~120-line where-clause. Whole files exist only to forward the list (solve_runtime_isolation_dispatch_runtime.rs:8-140 declares 36 params then calls the 41-param fn). `map_step` appears in 25 files of cas_solver_core; `map_unsupported_error` in 5 files across solver_core+solver. Every callback has shape FnMut(&mut T, ...) — i.e. they are methods on T in disguise (where-clause at solve_runtime_flow_isolation_default_routes.rs:96-120).
why: This is the solve pipeline's extension surface: adding one hook for a new equation family means editing a generic list + param list + where clause + forwarding call in a chain of wrapper files (measured up to 25 files for one callback). It directly taxes every universality cycle on solve, and the monomorphization of 35-generic fns is a compile-time hotspot.
fix: A context struct demonstrably collapses this: the repo already started one — dispatch_isolation_with_runtime_ctx_and_options_and_state (solve_runtime_isolation_dispatch_context_runtime.rs:11) packs scalars into RuntimeSolveCtx+SolverOptions and drops 41->23 params. Continue that seam incrementally: (1) group the pure Fn(&mut T,...) callbacks into a struct-of-callbacks (e.g. IsolationHooks<T,S,E> with named fields), one wrapper file per cycle, 0-delta huella; (2) later, replace &mut T + accessor closures with a SolveRuntime trait whose methods are the current callbacks. No big-bang: each wrapper file is independently convertible.
verify_reason: Independently re-measured every quantitative claim and traced liveness. The substance holds: crates/cas_solver_core/src has exactly 61 solve_runtime_*.rs files; the worst function (solve_runtime_flow_isolation_default_routes.rs:15) has 40 parameters and 34 generic type params with a ~34-line where-clause where every callback is FnMut(&mut T, ...) (claim said 41/35 — off-by-one counting noise). Dozens of functions exceed 10 params (40 measured vs 48 claimed; 27 vs 31 at >=15 — claim modestly overstated but same magnitude). map_step appears in exactly 25 files and map_unsupported_error in exactly 5, as claimed. The forwarding-only wrapper file (solve_runtime_isolation_dispatch_runtime.rs, 35-param fn calling the 40-param fn at line 137) and the already-started context-struct seam (dispatch_isolation_with_runtime_ctx_and_options_and_state at solve_runtime_isolation_dispatch_context_runtime.rs:11, packing SolverOptions + RuntimeSolveCtx, 21 params) both exist as described. Liveness verified: cas_solver's solve_inner (solve_core_runtime.rs:14, 'All public entry points delegate here') reaches the 40-param function through a 6-file wrapper chain (recursive_bound_runtime:229/286 -> bound_runtime:65 -> reentrant_context_runtime:107 -> context_runtime:113 -> dispatch_runtime:137 -> default_routes), so this is the live extension surface of every solve() call, and adding one hook really does require threading a new generic + param + where-bound + forwarding argument through each wrapper layer. P1 is appropriate for a foundation/coupling finding: not a wrong answer, but a measured recurring tax on solve universality work, and the repo's own partial migration to RuntimeSolveCtx corroborates both the problem and the proposed incremental fix.
extra_evidence: Corrected counts from my own parser (handles nested generics and -> in Fn types): worst fn = 40 params / 34 generics (not 41/35); fns >=10 params = 40 (not 48); >=15 params = 27 (not 31); dispatch_runtime wrapper = 35 params (not 36); context wrapper = 21 params (not 23). All off by small counting-methodology deltas that do not change severity or the fix. Additional evidence strengthening the finding: the live call chain has SIX stacked forwarding wrappers (solve_runtime_recursive_bound_runtime.rs:229 -> solve_runtime_isolation_dispatch_bound_runtime.rs:65 -> solve_runtime_isolation_dispatch_reentrant_context_runtime.rs:107 -> solve_runtime_isolation_dispatch_context_runtime.rs:113 -> solve_runtime_isolation_dispatch_runtime.rs:137 -> solve_runtime_flow_isolation_default_routes.rs:15), and the big fn immediately wraps 14+ of its callbacks in RefCell (default_routes.rs:126-140) just to re-share them — direct evidence the callbacks are morally methods on the state type, supporting the proposed struct-of-callbacks/trait fix.

title: Exact surd/constant sign-comparison algorithm implemented 3+ times across 3 crates, with capability drift already visible (phi, e/pi bounds)
evidence: The sign(A+B*sqrt(n)) squaring algorithm exists at: (a) solution_set.rs:117 sign_of_linear_surd ("Opposite signs: sign(p + q*sqrt(n)) = sign(q)*sign(q^2*n - p^2)"); (b) cas_math/src/root_forms.rs:1876 provable_sign_vs_zero (identical arithmetic, read both); (c) cas_solver/src/solve_backend_local.rs:1330 cmp_rational_to_quadratic_surd (same squaring, sign-tracking variant). N-th-root comparison duplicated too: solution_set.rs:230/286/306 (as_nth_root_value + compare_positive_nth_roots + compare_nth_root_surds) vs solve_backend_local.rs:1365 cmp_rational_to_nth_root. Drift is already real: the phi=1/2+1/2*sqrt(5) special-case exists at solution_set.rs:109-112 (as_surd_value) AND solve_backend_local.rs:1426, but NOT in the canonical root_forms::as_linear_surd — so isolation_utils.rs:31 is_known_negative (which delegates to provable_sign_vs_zero) cannot decide phi-forms while interval-endpoint ordering can. Also two parallel constant-bounds engines inside cas_math: root_forms.rs:1919 rational_bounds (private, e/pi to 3 decimals, no ln/trig) vs const_sign.rs:399 const_value_bounds (50dp bounds + ln/trig/nth-roots) — a comparison decidable by one fails the other.
why: This family IS the chokepoint the 2026-07-01 P0 campaign fixed ("surd-sign guard upgrade closes P0 families"): every capability upgrade must be applied to each copy or a sibling family reopens — exactly the recurring 'fix covers the named case, misses the sibling' bug shape from prior audits. Keep/drop decisions (solve_backend verification), interval-endpoint ordering (solution_set), and negativity guards (isolation_utils) can disagree on the same value today.
fix: Make cas_math canonical: (1) add phi to root_forms::as_linear_surd (deletes both downstream special-cases); (2) expose a value-level sign_of_linear_surd(p,q,n) and two-surd/nth-root comparators from root_forms and have solution_set.rs + solve_backend_local.rs call them; (3) have root_forms::rational_bounds delegate to const_sign::const_value_bounds. Each step is independently huella-checkable.
verify_reason: All three surd-sign implementations verified by reading: solution_set.rs:117 sign_of_linear_surd, root_forms.rs:1876 provable_sign_vs_zero (identical sign(B)*sign(B^2*n - A^2) arithmetic), and solve_backend_local.rs:1330 cmp_rational_to_quadratic_surd (same squaring, rational-vs-surd variant). N-th-root decomposition+comparison duplicated between solution_set.rs:230/286/306 and solve_backend_local.rs:1365/1436. Phi special-case confirmed present in both downstream copies (solution_set.rs:109-112, solve_backend_local.rs:1425-1429) and absent from canonical as_linear_surd (root_forms.rs:1739-1866 has no Constant::Phi). Two parallel bounds engines in cas_math confirmed (rational_bounds: private, e/pi at 3 decimals, no ln/trig/phi/sqrt; const_value_bounds: arbitrary-precision phi/ln/log/trig/sqrt). Duplication is NOT deliberate layering: both cas_solver_core and cas_solver depend on cas_math (Cargo.toml verified) and the duplicating files already call as_linear_surd/const_value_bounds from cas_math; no doc claims intentional duplication. Paths are hot: compare_values orders every interval endpoint in solve-inequality algebra; root_violates_required_condition is the extraneous-root keep/drop gate — both user-input reachable. Divergence is real, which per protocol sustains/raises severity: the two phi special-cases have themselves drifted (solve_backend handles Neg(Phi), solution_set only bare Phi). P1 stands for a dup with demonstrated drift in a family with documented P0 history.
extra_evidence: Two corrections/additions to the evidence: (1) The isolation_utils.rs:31 drift example is overstated — is_known_negative has a second disjunct (lines 42-43) falling back to cas_math::const_sign::provable_const_sign, whose const_value_bounds handles Phi via arbitrary-precision sqrt bounds (const_sign.rs:419-424), so strictly-signed phi-forms like 1-phi ARE decidable there; only exact-zero phi combinations (phi^2-phi-1) remain undecidable by that path. (2) Additional drift the claim missed: solve_backend_local.rs bound_surd (1425-1429) handles both Phi and Neg(Phi), while solution_set.rs as_surd_value (109-112) handles only bare Phi — Neg(Phi) recurses into as_linear_surd's Neg branch which does not know Phi and returns None, so the two special-cases have diverged from each other, not just from the canonical decomposition. (3) A fourth composed sign oracle exists at solve_backend_local.rs:402-423, which chains provable_sign_vs_zero -> provable_sign_vs_zero_const_radicand -> an inline const_value_bounds straddle check that re-implements const_sign::provable_const_sign (const_sign.rs:595-599). Proposed fix step (1) (add Phi to as_linear_surd) must cover Neg(Phi) via the existing Neg recursion, which it would automatically once Phi is a leaf case.

title: Unbounded BigInt trial division in extract_root_factor hangs EvaluatePowerRule on large bases
evidence: root_forms.rs:2488-2506: `let mut d = BigInt::from(3); while &d * &d <= n_abs { (&n_abs % &d) ...; d += 2; }` — no divisor cap, no bit-size gate, no deadline poll. Caller power_eval_support.rs:161-166 (try_rewrite_evaluate_power_expr) runs it on EVERY Number^(p/q) with u32 denominator, via cas_engine/src/rules/exponents/power_rules.rs:535-546 (EvaluatePowerRule, a core Pow-bucket rule). MEASURED: `timeout 10 cas_cli eval "1234567890123456789012345678901234567891^(1/2)"` spins 10s CPU until killed; a 35-term telescoping fraction sum runs >5min (killed) while 15/25 terms take 0.7s/2.5s, and a 5s `sample` profile places 3800/3800 samples under try_rewrite_evaluate_power_expr → num_bigint div_rem/add — exactly this loop. Sibling unbounded loops: number_theory_support.rs:642,968,1243; logarithm_inverse_support.rs:503.
why: The rule loop's cooperative deadline (rule_application.rs time_budget_exceeded) cannot preempt inside one rule.apply(), so any input whose constant-folding produces a large non-smooth integer under ^(1/q) hangs the engine — audit sweeps and universality campaigns stall on inputs that should decline honestly; a 40-digit semiprime needs ~10^20 iterations (effectively forever).
fix: Early-out in extract_root_factor when n.bits() exceeds a threshold (~96-128) returning (1, n) — the surd stays unfactored, which is sound — or cap the trial divisor (~2^20) and fold the remainder into `inside`. Huella-risk near zero: affected inputs currently never terminate. Apply the same cap to the 4 sibling loops.
verify_reason: Reproduced and root-caused at HEAD ab5587aa1. The unbounded odd-trial-division loop exists exactly as claimed at crates/cas_math/src/root_forms.rs:2489-2507 with no divisor cap, bit gate, or deadline. The caller chain is live and hot: power_eval_support.rs:161-166 (try_rewrite_evaluate_power_expr) runs it on every Number^(p/q), invoked by EvaluatePowerRule (power_rules.rs:534-549), a core POW-bucket CORE|POST rule registered in the default simplifier (cas_engine/src/rules/exponents/mod.rs:34). Empirically: `cas_cli eval "1234567890123456789012345678901234567891^(1/2)"` spins 100% CPU for 10s and 30s external timeouts with no internal abort; a 3s `sample` profile puts 2295/2295 relevant samples in try_rewrite_evaluate_power_expr -> extract_root_factor -> BigUint rem. The no-preemption claim is structurally verified: engine/transform/rule_application.rs polls time_budget_exceeded() at line 100 BEFORE each rule.apply() at line 113, so the budget cannot interrupt inside one apply — confirmed by the 30s uninterrupted spin. Control case 12345678917^(1/2) returns instantly, so only large non-smooth bases (directly typeable user input) trigger the hang; a 40-digit semiprime needs ~10^19 iterations, effectively forever. Sibling unbounded loops confirmed present at number_theory_support.rs:641/967/1242 and logarithm_inverse_support.rs:502. P1 severity stands: valid user input hangs the engine indefinitely on a default-registered core rule.
extra_evidence: Minor path corrections only: the budget poll lives at crates/cas_engine/src/engine/transform/rule_application.rs (not a top-level rule_application.rs), and the loop starts at line 2489 (claim said ~2490). Profile file: scratchpad/sample.txt. The 35-term telescoping-sum repro was not re-run (unnecessary — the direct 40-digit input already proves user reachability and the profiler pins the exact loop).

title: Per-node soundness backstop walks the whole subtree (with multipoly zero-proofs) even when no rewrite occurred
evidence: transform/mod.rs:326-345: after `apply_rules`, `rewrite_unsoundly_drops_nonfinite_in_domain(ctx, expr_with_simplified_children, result, ...)` is called with NO `result != input` guard. arithmetic_cancel_support.rs:1689-1721 clause 1 always executes `expr_carries_undefined_in_domain(before)` — a full recursive subtree walk (1746-1763), plus a second walk when the node is Add/Sub (clause 2, 1718-1720). Inside the walk every Div runs `is_provably_zero(den)` (995-1070) which for Add/Sub denominators builds a multipoly with budget max_terms=256 / max_total_degree=64 / max_pow_exp=12. No ExprId memoization despite the hash-consed immutable arena, so the same denominators are re-proved at every ancestor's visit, every pass, every phase.
why: This makes each simplify pass O(sum of subtree sizes) ≈ O(n·depth) walks plus repeated bounded-but-expensive multipoly expansions in the single hottest loop of the engine; the common case (fixpoint reached, apply_rules returns unchanged) pays the full price. Every new rule/front added by a universality campaign multiplies passes over this tax.
fix: One-line early-out: skip the backstop when `result == expr_with_simplified_children` (the backstop can only fire when a rewrite happened). Then memoize expr_carries_undefined/nonfinite per ExprId in the transformer (sound: arena nodes are immutable). Huella-safe: identical results.
verify_reason: Every factual claim verified by direct code reading, and the perf impact verified empirically by profiling the release binary. transform/mod.rs:326-345 calls rewrite_unsoundly_drops_nonfinite_in_domain unconditionally after apply_rules with no result!=input guard; arithmetic_cancel_support.rs clause 1 (line 1706) always executes a full unmemoized recursive subtree walk (expr_carries_undefined_in_domain, 1738-1763), plus a second walk for Add/Sub nodes (1718-1720); every Div in the walk runs is_provably_zero (995-1068) which builds a multipoly with budget 256/64/12 for Add/Sub denominators (1040-1044); is_structurally_undefined additionally runs is_provably_zero on every Pow base. No ExprId memoization exists — the transformer's per-pass cache dedups only the transform result, so a denominator at depth d is re-proved at all d ancestor walks. The call site is in transform_expr_recursive, the engine's per-node core rewrite loop (caller chain: Simplifier::simplify_* -> transform_expr_recursive -> backstop), i.e. the hottest path. When result == input the function provably returns false (both clauses need carries(before) && !carries(after), impossible for identical ids) and it is pure (&Context), so the proposed early-out is behavior-identical. Profiling (macOS sample, 1ms, release): backstop inclusive = 3.1% of runtime on a nested-division stress (152/4886 samples), 5% on a 300-term sum of i/(x^2+i) (125/2494), and 20.6% on a 30-term sum of i/((x+y+1)^12+i) (686/3324) — an unexceptional input class where the quadratic re-proving of multipoly denominators dominates. Hot path + measurable common-case tax reaching ~21% + trivially sound one-line fix justifies P1.
extra_evidence: Profiles taken with `sample` on target/release/cas_cli (built today, HEAD ab5587aa1). rewrite_unsoundly_drops_nonfinite_in_domain is non-recursive so summing its call-tree line counts gives valid inclusive samples (possible undercount if partially inlined, so the measured fractions are a floor). Inputs: (1) 11-level nested (e+i)/(x^2+ix+(i+1)) — 9.8s, 3.1%; (2) sum_{i=1..299} i/(x^2+i) — 2.8s, 5.0%; (3) sum_{i=1..30} i/((x+y+1)^12+i) — 4.6s, 20.6%. Also verified there is a second unguarded-looking call site at orchestrator.rs:26315 (root-shortcut dispatch, once per shortcut — not hot, not part of this finding). Fix note: the early-out `result == expr_with_simplified_children -> skip` is provably behavior-identical (pure function of (&Context, before, after) with before==after always yields false); the follow-up ExprId memoization of expr_carries_undefined/nonfinite is sound because arena nodes are immutable, but the memo must be keyed per (ExprId, ValueDomainMode) and cleared per Context if reused across sessions.

title: Global SubSelfToZeroRule/AddInverseRule run the full carries-nonfinite subtree walk BEFORE the trivial Sub/Add shape check, on every node of every kind
evidence: arithmetic_cancel_support.rs:1766-1778: `try_rewrite_sub_self_zero_expr` first line is `if expr_carries_nonfinite_or_undefined(ctx, expr) { return None; }` (full subtree walk incl. is_provably_zero multipoly on Div denominators) and only THEN `match_sub_self_semantic_expr` rejects non-Sub nodes; same order in try_rewrite_add_inverse_zero_expr (1781-1793). Both rules are GLOBAL: define_rule! forms without targets (rules/arithmetic.rs:30483-30500 SubSelfToZeroRule, 30565+ AddInverseRule) default to target_types()=None (macros.rs:446-470, rule.rs:414-416), so the global-rules loop (rule_application.rs:350) attempts them at every node visit — Numbers, Variables, Pows, Functions — in Core AND PostCleanup phases, every fixpoint iteration.
why: Two O(subtree) walks per node visit baked into the rule-application loop = another O(n²)-per-pass tax identical in shape to finding 2, paid even by leaf nodes that can never match a - a.
fix: Hoist the shape check: match Expr::Sub (resp. add-inverse shape) FIRST, then run the carries walk only on matched nodes (the walk is only needed for soundness when the cancellation would actually fire). Two-line reorder, huella-safe.
verify_reason: Every factual assertion checks out. (1) Order: arithmetic_cancel_support.rs:1770 and :1785 run the full-subtree expr_carries_nonfinite_or_undefined walk BEFORE the O(1) shape rejection (match_sub_self_semantic_expr line 79 rejects non-Sub; match_add_inverse_expr line 142 rejects non-Add). (2) Walk cost: lines 1622-1647 recurse the whole subtree, per-node is_structurally_undefined includes trig-pole rational-pi checks, and every Div denominator triggers is_provably_zero which attempts a budgeted multipoly expansion (max_terms=256, max_pow_exp=12, lines 1034-1049) — re-run once per ancestor node visit. (3) Global: both define_rule! forms used (macros.rs 3-arg and 3-arg+priority) omit target_types, defaulting to None (rule.rs:414-416); add_rule (simplifier.rs:479-488) puts None-target rules in global_rules. (4) Hot path traced: register_default_rules (simplifier.rs:399) → arithmetic::register (arithmetic.rs:39171/39181) → transform_expr_recursive → apply_rules global loop (rule_application.rs:350-363) invoked at EVERY node visit including Number/Variable leaves (transform/mod.rs:275-276 + apply_rules call), in both CORE and POST phase slots (default allowed_phases CORE|POST). (5) Proposed fix is behavior-preserving: both matchers are pure &Context predicates, so hoisting the shape check yields identical Some/None outcomes — huella-safe. P1 stands: this is the engine's hottest loop and the wasted walk has a heavyweight constant (multipoly per Div-with-Add-denominator per ancestor).
extra_evidence: Two refinements that do not demote: (a) leaf nodes pay only O(1) for the walk (single non-recursing match arm), so the "paid even by leaf nodes" framing overstates the leaf cost — the real tax is interior non-Sub/non-Add nodes (Mul/Div/Pow/Function) where two O(subtree) walks are pure waste; (b) a per-pass ExprId memo cache (engine/transform/mod.rs:267) means each unique node id is transformed once per pass, so the aggregate tax is O(sum of subtree sizes) ≈ O(n·depth) per pass rather than naively per fixpoint iteration — still O(n²) on the left-deep binary Add/Sub chains the parser produces, which matches the claimed shape.

title: Semantic-equality fallback clones the ENTIRE Context arena on every Div-vs-non-Div comparison, before any cheap shape gate
evidence: semantic_equality.rs:243-255: `fn div_add_common_factor_rewrite_matches` first line is `let mut scratch = self.context.clone();` then calls `try_rewrite_div_add_common_factor_from_den_expr`, whose OWN first checks (div_add_common_factor_from_den_support.rs:25-33) are cheap read-only early-outs (`as_div`, numerator must be Add/Sub with >=2 terms). The clone happens before those gates. Trigger arms are the catch-alls at semantic_equality.rs:187-189: `(Expr::Div(_,_), _) => self.div_add_common_factor_rewrite_matches(a, b)` — i.e. ANY comparison of a Div node against a non-Div/non-Number (e.g. `x/2` vs `sin(x)`) pays a full arena clone (Vec<Expr> with BigRational/Vec payloads + FxHashMap interner + SymbolTable, per expression.rs:142-153). This checker is the workhorse of cas_solver's derive matching: strong_target_match/factors_match (crates/cas_solver/src/derive/match_support.rs:108-116, 318-326) call it 2x per invocation; 514 call sites of the match functions across derive_command.rs + derive/*.rs (273 in derive/trig.rs alone), including O(n^2) per-pair use inside commutative_add/mul_multiset_match (match_support.rs:233-249, 268-284).
why: The clone cost scales with the session arena, and the recursion can hit the Div arm multiple times per single are_equal call; inside the O(n^2) multiset matcher this becomes O(pairs x arena) allocation. Slow derive/solve paths convert directly into budget-exhaustion declines and hangs (cf. the C5 hang lore), i.e. capability loss for a universality campaign — and the arena only grows during a session, so it compounds.
fix: Hoist the read-only shape gate before cloning: in div_add_common_factor_rewrite_matches, return false immediately unless `as_div(source)` succeeds AND the numerator is Add/Sub (both checks need only &Context). Only then clone the scratch context. Huella-safe: pure reordering of existing checks, identical results.
verify_reason: All claim elements verified by direct code reading. semantic_equality.rs:244 clones the entire Context (Vec<Expr> + FxHashMap interner + SymbolTable, expression.rs:142-153) as the FIRST statement, before the callee's cheap read-only gates (div_add_common_factor_from_den_support.rs:25-34: as_div takes &Context, numerator-must-be-Add/Sub is a matches! on ctx.get). Catch-all arms at semantic_equality.rs:187-189 send every Div-vs-non-Div/non-Number comparison there, and the checker's recursion (Add/Mul/Function arms) can hit the Div arm multiple times per are_equal call. Hot path confirmed and is actually STRONGER than claimed: besides the cited cas_solver derive matchers (strong_target_match/factors_match at match_support.rs:108-116/318-326, including O(n^2) multiset loops at 233-249/268-284), the checker runs in cas_engine/src/engine/transform/rule_application.rs:141-148 on EVERY non-didactic rewrite accepted by the main simplifier loop — the canonical rule-application hot path — plus equivalence.rs:118/148, orchestrator.rs:671, and cas_solver_core step_semantic.rs:24/step_productivity.rs:72,83. Any Div-to-non-Div rewrite (fraction cancellation, div→mul normalization) pays an O(arena) clone per comparison, and the arena grows monotonically per session. Proposed fix is valid and huella-safe: both hoistable gates need only &Context, which the checker already holds.
extra_evidence: Minor count correction: measured 474 combined strong_target_match/factors_match references in cas_solver (claim said 514) and 241 in derive/trig.rs (claim said 273) — same order of magnitude, verdict unaffected. Additional evidence strengthening severity: rule_application.rs:140-148 performs checker.are_equal(expr_id, final_expr) for every rewrite produced by any rule in the simplify loop (skipped only for didactic rules), so the clone triggers far more broadly than the derive-matching paths cited in the claim. Note the source passed to div_add_common_factor_rewrite_matches is always the Div side (arms 187/189), so as_div always succeeds; the load-bearing hoisted gate is the numerator Add/Sub check — a single read-only matches!(self.context.get(num), Expr::Add(..)|Expr::Sub(..)) suffices to skip the clone in the overwhelmingly common false case (e.g. x/2 vs sin(x)).

title: Four superseded LaTeX renderer structs (~110 lines) kept alongside the live PathHighlightedLatexRenderer
evidence: SimpleLatexRenderer (:1349), HighlightedLatexRenderer (:1369), HintedLatexRenderer (:1394), FullLatexRenderer (:1422) each have exactly 2 in-file occurrences (definition + trait impl) and zero references anywhere else in the workspace (incl. tests/benches). lib.rs:43 re-exports only `latex_core::PathHighlightedLatexRenderer`, which has 23 external construction sites (cas_didactic timeline/step renderers). The dead four carry stale semantics (HintedLatexRenderer returns the root DisplayContext 'for all' ids — a shortcut the live renderer replaced).
why: Superseded implementations both still present: they are in a `pub mod`, so they look like supported API for LaTeX rendering (a hot area for didactic output work) and encode outdated hint/highlight semantics that would produce wrong-looking steps if someone picks them up.
fix: Delete the four structs + impls (lines ~1344-1447). No re-exports or callers exist, so the change is huella-safe.
verify_reason: Core dead-code claim fully verified: the four structs (latex_core.rs:1349/1369/1394/1422, block ~1344-1447) have exactly 2 in-file occurrences each and zero references anywhere else in the workspace (all file types incl. tests/benches/scripts; no glob imports, no trait-object or macro dispatch; lib.rs:43 re-exports only PathHighlightedLatexRenderer). Deletion is caller-free. However, the claimed hazard is wrong: HintedLatexRenderer's return-root-DisplayContext-for-all-ids shortcut is NOT stale semantics the live renderer replaced — the live, externally-used LaTeXExprWithHints (latex.rs:99-101) and LaTeXExprHighlightedWithHints (latex_highlight.rs) have the identical shortcut. All four dead structs are exact semantic duplicates of still-live wrappers (LaTeXExpr, LaTeXExprHighlighted, LaTeXExprWithHints, LaTeXExprHighlightedWithHints), so no divergent/latent-inconsistency risk exists. With the wrong-output hazard refuted, this is pure redundant dead API surface: safe cleanup, P3 not P2.
extra_evidence: Duplicate mapping (dead → live equivalent): SimpleLatexRenderer ≡ LaTeXExpr (latex.rs:19-32); HintedLatexRenderer ≡ LaTeXExprWithHints (latex.rs:84-104, same get_display_hint(_id) -> Some(self.hints)); HighlightedLatexRenderer ≡ LaTeXExprHighlighted (latex_highlight.rs:125); FullLatexRenderer ≡ LaTeXExprHighlightedWithHints (latex_highlight.rs:162). The live equivalents have ~113 external usage lines across the workspace. Only external latex_core:: paths outside cas_formatter/src are trait imports of LaTeXRenderer (latex.rs:9, latex_highlight.rs:10), which other live implementors need, so delete only the four structs+impls (lines ~1344-1447, 104 lines), keep the trait. Proposed fix stands as huella-safe.
